label: CIR / Credit-Report Correction Delay
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I filed a CIR / credit-report dispute with my bank on 1 January 2026.
The corrected data was sent/resolved on 20 February 2026.
Please calculate compensation as Rs 100 per day beyond the 30-day correction window.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because the bank took longer than the allowed 30 days to correct your credit report dispute. Since you filed on 1 January 2026 and the correction was completed on 20 February 2026, the delay exceeded the 30-day window. The bank should pay you Rs 2000 as compensation for this delay. This amount is fully borne by the bank, with no liability on your part. The compensation was calculated based on the number of days beyond the 30-day correction period, at Rs 100 per day, as per RBI guidelines.

label: Card to Card Transfer Failure (T+1)
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I tried transferring from one card to another card on 10 January 2026 for ₹12,000.
The beneficiary card did not get credited and it was resolved only on 12 January 2026.
Assume T+1 applies, so compute Rs 100 per day beyond T+1.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because your card-to-card transfer of ₹12,000 on 10 January 2026 was not credited on time and was only resolved on 12 January 2026. The bank should pay you ₹100 as compensation for the delay beyond the expected T+1 day processing time. There is no liability on your part, and the entire compensation amount is borne by the bank. The compensation was calculated based on the delay in crediting your beneficiary card after the usual one-day processing period. Since no specific bank name is mentioned, there is no additional note about the bank’s own policy.

label: Cheque Lost in Transit
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I deposited an outstation cheque of ₹20,000 on 1 January 2026.
The cheque was lost in transit and the actual credit/information came only on 25 January 2026.
Cheque collection TAT is 10 days and cheque collection interest rate is 3% per annum.
SB interest rate is 3% per annum.
My documented costs (stop payment, duplicate, etc.) are ₹400, but the rule caps documented costs at ₹250.
Compute compensation using: interest beyond TAT + interest for extra 15 days at SB rate + capped costs.

api_response.other_info: Thank you for the details provided. To help calculate your compensation accurately, could you please confirm the exact date when the ₹20,000 was credited to your account? Also, please confirm the date you reported the cheque as lost or initiated the stop payment process.

label: Cheque Paid After Stop-Payment
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I gave stop-payment instruction for a cheque of ₹25,000 before the bank presented it.
However, the cheque was still paid and the credit/payment posted to my account on 10 January 2026.
The original debit date was 1 January 2026.
SB interest rate is 3% per annum.
Downstream charges/interest impact (min-balance penalty, extra interest impact, etc.) is ₹500.
Compute compensation as: cheque amount reversal + value-dated interest impact + downstream charges impact.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because you gave a stop-payment instruction before the cheque was presented, but the bank still processed the payment on 10 January 2026. The bank will pay you a total compensation of ₹27,349.32. This amount includes the ₹25,000 cheque amount reversed, plus interest calculated for the period the funds were wrongly debited, and an additional ₹500 to cover any downstream charges or interest impacts caused by this error. The compensation covers the financial inconvenience and any penalties you faced due to the delay. Since there is no mention of any customer liability, the full amount is borne by the bank. The bank’s calculation follows RBI guidelines to ensure you are made whole for the mistake without you incurring any loss.

label: Credit Card - Delayed Closure
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I requested closure of my credit card on 1 January 2026.
There were no outstanding dues.
The bank failed to close the card within the required T+7 working days.
It got closed later, and the delay beyond T+7 working days is 5 working days.
Compute compensation as Rs 500 per day beyond T+7.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because your credit card closure was delayed beyond the required 7 working days after your request, even though there were no outstanding dues. The bank should pay you Rs 2,500 as compensation, which is Rs 500 for each of the 5 working days of delay beyond the T+7 period. This amount is fully borne by the bank. The compensation was calculated based on the rule that for delayed credit card closure, the bank pays a fixed amount per day of delay after the allowed time frame. Since the bank's name is not provided, there is no additional note about any specific bank policy.

label: Credit Card - Issued Without Consent
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. A credit card was issued and activated without my written digital consent.
I disputed the charges, and I did not use the card at all.
Total charges reversed due to the dispute are ₹1,500.
Compute compensation as 2 × charges reversed.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because a credit card was issued and activated without your written digital consent, which is not allowed. Since you disputed the charges and did not use the card, the bank has calculated compensation as twice the amount of the reversed charges. Therefore, the bank should pay you ₹3,000 as compensation. There is no liability on your part. The compensation amount reflects the bank’s responsibility to make up for the inconvenience and any potential risk caused by issuing the card without your consent. Since no specific bank name was provided, there is no additional note about the bank’s own policy.

label: Delay in Return of Loan Security Docs
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I fully repaid my loan and repayment was completed on 1 January 2026.
For my product, the allowed return period is 15 working days.
The bank returned the title/security documents after an additional 20 working days beyond the allowed period.
This is NOT a premium product, so it is Rs 100 per week (not per day).
Cap amount for this compensation is ₹5,000.
Compute the compensation accordingly.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because the bank returned your loan security documents 20 working days later than the allowed 15 working days after your loan repayment was completed. Since this is not a premium product, the compensation rate is Rs 100 per week of delay, with a maximum cap of Rs 5,000. The bank will pay you Rs 300 as compensation for the delay. This amount reflects the delay period beyond the allowed time, calculated on a weekly basis, and capped as per the bank’s policy. There is no liability on your part, and the bank fully bears this compensation cost.

label: ECS/Direct Debit - Failed/Delayed Execution
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. My valid ECS/Direct Debit mandate was scheduled to execute on 1 January 2026.
Due to bank failure, it actually got executed on 6 January 2026.
The amount involved was ₹10,000.
SB interest rate is 3% per annum.
Customer penalties/charges at other banks due to the failure are ₹500.
Compute compensation as SB-rate interest for delay period (clamped between ₹100 and ₹1,000) + customer penalties.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because your ECS/Direct Debit payment was delayed by the bank, causing the transaction to happen 5 days later than scheduled. The bank should pay you a total of ₹910.96 as compensation. This amount includes the interest calculated at the savings bank rate for the delay period, adjusted to fall between ₹100 and ₹1,000, plus the ₹500 penalty charges you incurred at other banks due to this delay. The compensation covers the inconvenience and financial impact caused by the late debit. The bank bears the full compensation amount, and there is no liability on your part. The calculation follows RBI guidelines to ensure fair compensation for delayed ECS/Direct Debit transactions.

label: Erroneous Debit (Bank Error)
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I was wrongly debited/duplicate debited by the bank for ₹30,000.
The bank reversed it later, but I suffered an interest loss of ₹1,000 and downstream charges reversal of ₹500.
This involved a bank staff error / staff-fraud case, so apply the +1% staff-fraud uplift.
Compute compensation as interest loss + downstream reversal + 1% uplift in staff-fraud cases.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because the bank made an error by wrongly debiting your account and later reversing it. The bank acknowledges the interest loss of ₹1,000 and the downstream charges reversal of ₹500 you suffered. Additionally, since this involved a staff error or fraud, a 1% uplift was applied as per the rules. The total compensation amount the bank will pay you is ₹1,510. This amount fully covers your losses, and the bank bears the entire cost. The compensation calculation considered the delay and losses you faced due to the erroneous debit and included the extra uplift for staff-related issues to fairly compensate you.

label: Failed IMPS (T+1)
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. My IMPS transaction of ₹8,000 failed (beneficiary not credited) and the amount was debited on 10 January 2026.
The funds were resolved (auto-reversal / credit) only on 12 January 2026.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because your IMPS transaction failed and the amount was credited back to you later than the expected T+1 day. The bank should pay you Rs 100 as compensation for the delay beyond the allowed time. There is no liability on your part, and the full compensation amount is borne by the bank. The compensation was calculated based on the delay in refunding your money after the transaction failure, applying Rs 100 per day beyond the T+1 day limit.

label: FD - Failed Action on Maturity Instruction
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I provided maturity instruction for a Fixed Deposit before the cut-off.
The bank failed to execute it properly, and I suffered lost interest of ₹2,500 compared to the intended outcome.
Compute compensation as the lost interest amount.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because you gave the maturity instruction for your Fixed Deposit before the cut-off time, but the bank did not execute it properly, causing you to lose ₹2,500 in interest. The bank should pay you the full amount of ₹2,500 as compensation. This amount reflects the interest you missed out on due to the bank’s failure to act on your instruction in time. The calculation follows RBI guidelines that ensure you are compensated for any loss caused by the bank’s delay or error, without you bearing any liability. If you have a specific bank in mind, their own policies might offer additional benefits, but based on RBI rules, this is the fair compensation for your case.

Since there is no mention of any customer liability or bank-specific policy, the full compensation amount is borne by the bank.

label: Locker - Loss Due to Bank Negligence
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. My locker contents were lost due to bank negligence at the bank premises.
Annual locker rent is ₹200.
The policy cap for locker loss compensation is ₹25,000.
Compute compensation as 100 × annual locker rent, capped by the cap.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because the loss of your locker contents happened due to the bank’s negligence at their premises. According to the policy, compensation is calculated as 100 times the annual locker rent, but it cannot exceed the maximum cap set by the bank. In your case, the annual rent is ₹200, so 100 times that is ₹20,000, which is below the cap of ₹25,000. Therefore, the bank will pay you ₹20,000 as compensation. There is no amount you need to bear. The calculation follows the standard rule that compensates based on the rent multiple, limited by the maximum cap, without involving any delay or interest adjustments. Since the bank name is not specified, there are no additional bank-specific notes to mention.

label: Violation by Bank's Agent
user_story:
Today is 8 February 2026. I am complaining about improper conduct / violation by the bank's agent (DSA/courier/representative) within bank scope.
My actual direct financial loss is ₹10,000.
Compute compensation as the actual direct financial loss only.

api_response.other_info: You are eligible for compensation because the complaint involves improper conduct or violation by the bank’s agent, which caused you a direct financial loss of ₹10,000. The bank will pay you the full amount of ₹10,000 as compensation. Since this is a direct loss caused by the bank’s representative, the entire compensation amount is borne by the bank. The compensation was calculated based on the actual financial loss you suffered, without any deductions or delays considered, as per RBI guidelines for such cases. If you have a specific bank in mind, their own policies might add further benefits, but here the standard RBI rules apply.
